stream.h(3) Library Functions Manual stream.h(3)

NAME

stream.h - Multi-client video mixing and ASCII frame generation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


char * create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client (const char *target_client_id, unsigned short width, unsigned short height, bool wants_stretch, size_t *out_size, bool *out_grid_changed, int *out_sources_count)
Generate personalized ASCII frame for a specific client. bool any_clients_sending_video (void)
Check if any connected clients are currently sending video. int queue_audio_for_client (client_info_t *client, const void *audio_data, size_t data_size)
Queue ASCII frame for delivery to specific client.

Variables


atomic_t g_should_exit
Global application exit flag (shared across all modes).

Detailed Description

Multi-client video mixing and ASCII frame generation.

Definition in file stream.h.

Function Documentation

bool any_clients_sending_video (void )

Check if any connected clients are currently sending video. This function scans all active clients to determine if at least one is sending video frames. Used by render threads to avoid generating frames when no video sources are available (e.g., during webcam warmup).

LOCK OPTIMIZATION: Uses atomic reads only, no rwlock acquisition This is safe because client_id, active, and is_sending_video are all atomics

Returns

true if at least one client has is_sending_video flag set, false otherwise

Definition at line 1321 of file stream.c.

1321                                      {
1322   // LOCK OPTIMIZATION: Don't acquire rwlock - all fields we access are atomic
1323   // client_id, active, is_sending_video are all atomic variables
1324 
1325   // Iterate through all client slots
1326   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
1327     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
1328 
1329     // Skip uninitialized clients (atomic read)
1330     if (client->client_id[0] == '\0') {
1331       continue;
1332     }
1333 
1334     // Check if client is active and sending video (both atomic reads)
1335     bool is_active = atomic_load_bool(&client->active);
1336     bool is_sending = atomic_load_bool(&client->is_sending_video);
1337 
1338     if (is_active && is_sending) {
1339       return true;
1340     }
1341   }
1342 
1343   return false;
1344 }




References client_info::active, atomic_load_bool(),
    client_info::client_id, g_client_manager,
    client_info::is_sending_video, and MAX_CLIENTS.


Referenced by client_video_render_thread().






char
  * create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client (const char * target_client_id, unsigned
  short width, unsigned short height, bool wants_stretch, size_t *
  out_size, bool * out_grid_changed, int * out_sources_count)


Generate personalized ASCII frame for a specific client. This is
    the core video mixing function that creates customized ASCII art frames for
    individual clients. It collects video from all active clients, creates an
    appropriate grid layout, and converts to ASCII using the target client's
    terminal capabilities.










ALGORITHM
  OVERVIEW: 



  
1.

  
FRAME COLLECTION PHASE:

  
 •

  
Scan all active clients for available video frames

  
 •

  
Use double-buffer system to get latest frames

  
 •

  
Implement aggressive frame dropping under load (maintains real-time)

  
 •

  
Always use the most current frame for professional-grade quality

  
2.

  
LAYOUT CALCULATION PHASE:

  
 •

  
Determine grid dimensions based on active client count

  
 •

  
Calculate cell sizes with aspect ratio preservation

  
 •

  
Handle special cases: single client, multiple clients

  
3.

  
COMPOSITE GENERATION PHASE:

  
 •

  
Create composite image with appropriate dimensions

  
 •

  
Place each client's video in grid cell with proper scaling

  
 •

  
Support both normal and half-block rendering modes

  
4.

  
ASCII CONVERSION PHASE:

  
 •

  
Convert composite to ASCII using client capabilities

  
 •

  
Apply client-specific palette and color settings

  
 •

  
Generate ANSI escape sequences if supported








PERFORMANCE
  OPTIMIZATIONS: 


FRAME BUFFER MANAGEMENT:


    
  
  • Aggressive frame dropping: always read latest available
    • 
  
  • Double-buffer system: ensures smooth frame delivery
    • 
  
  • Buffer pool allocation: reduces malloc/free overhead
    • 
  
  • Zero-copy operations where possible
    • 



CONCURRENCY OPTIMIZATIONS:


    
  
  • Reader locks on client manager (allows parallel execution)
    • 
  
  • Double-buffer atomic operations (lock-free access)
    • 
  
  • Atomic snapshots of client state
    • 
  
  • Thread-safe buffer operations
    • 







CLIENT
  CAPABILITY INTEGRATION: 


TERMINAL AWARENESS:


    
  
  • Respects client's color depth capabilities
    • 
  
  • Uses client's preferred ASCII palette
    • 
  
  • Handles UTF-8 vs ASCII-only terminals
    • 
  
  • Supports half-block mode for 2x vertical resolution
    • 



RENDERING MODES SUPPORTED:


    
  
  • RENDER_MODE_FOREGROUND: Traditional ASCII art
    • 
  
  • RENDER_MODE_BACKGROUND: Inverted colors for better contrast
    • 
  
  • RENDER_MODE_HALF_BLOCK: Unicode half-blocks for 2x resolution
    • 







ERROR
  HANDLING STRATEGY: 


    
  
  • Invalid dimensions: Logged and frame generation aborted
    • 
  
  • Memory allocation failures: Graceful degradation, cleanup resources
    • 
  
  • Client disconnections: Skip client in current frame, continue
    • 
  
  • Buffer overruns: Frame dropping maintains real-time performance
    • 
  
  • Capability missing: Wait for capabilities before generating frames
    • 







MEMORY MANAGEMENT: 


    
  
  • All image operations use buffer pool allocation
    • 
  
  • Frame data is properly freed after use
    • 
  
  • Cached frames use deep copies (prevent corruption)
    • 
  
  • Automatic cleanup on function exit
    • 



Parameters


target_client_id Client who will receive this
  customized frame


width Terminal width in characters for this client


height Terminal height in characters for this client


wants_stretch Unused parameter (aspect ratio always preserved)


out_size Pointer to store resulting ASCII frame size


Returns


Allocated ASCII frame string (caller must free), or NULL
  on error


Note


This function is called at 60fps per client by render
  threads



Generated frame is customized for target client's capabilities




Memory allocated from buffer pool - caller must free properly




Warning


Function performs extensive validation - invalid input
  returns NULL



Client capabilities must be received before frame generation




See also


ascii_convert_with_capabilities() For ASCII
  conversion implementation



framebuffer_read_multi_frame() For frame buffer
    operations




calculate_fit_dimensions_pixel() For aspect ratio
    calculations




Definition at line 958 of file stream.c.


960                                                                   {
961   (void)wants_stretch; // Unused - we always handle aspect ratio ourselves
962 
963   // Register stream atomics with named debug registry
964   static bool stream_atomics_registered = false;
965   if (!stream_atomics_registered) {
966     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_previous_active_video_count, "server_video_source_count_for_layout_detection", NULL);
967     stream_atomics_registered = true;
968   }
969 
970   uint64_t frame_gen_start_ns = time_get_ns();
971 
972   // Initialize output parameters
973   if (out_grid_changed) {
974     *out_grid_changed = false;
975   }
976   if (out_sources_count) {
977     *out_sources_count = 0;
978   }
979 
980   if (!out_size || width == 0 || height == 0) {
981     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
982               "Invalid parameters for create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client: width=%u, height=%u, out_size=%p", width,
983               height, out_size);
984     return NULL;
985   }
986 
987   // Collect all active clients and their image sources
988   image_source_t sources[MAX_CLIENTS];
989   uint64_t collect_start_ns = time_get_ns();
990   int source_count = collect_video_sources(sources, MAX_CLIENTS);
991   uint64_t collect_end_ns = time_get_ns();
992 
993   // Count sources that actually have video data
994   int sources_with_video = 0;
995   for (int i = 0; i < source_count; i++) {
996     if (sources[i].has_video && sources[i].image) {
997       sources_with_video++;
998     }
999   }
1000 
1001   static uint64_t last_detailed_log = 0;
1002   uint64_t now_ns = collect_end_ns;
1003   if (now_ns - last_detailed_log > 333 * NS_PER_MS_INT) { // Log every 333ms (3x per second)
1004     last_detailed_log = now_ns;
1005     log_info("FRAME_GEN_START: target_client=%u sources=%d collect=%.1fms", target_client_id, sources_with_video,
1006              (collect_end_ns - collect_start_ns) / NS_PER_MS);
1007   }
1008 
1009   // Return the source count for debugging/tracking
1010   if (out_sources_count) {
1011     *out_sources_count = sources_with_video;
1012   }
1013 
1014   // GRID LAYOUT CHANGE DETECTION:
1015   // Check if the number of active video sources has changed
1016   // NOTE: We only UPDATE the count and SIGNAL the change via out parameter
1017   // Broadcasting CLEAR_CONSOLE must happen AFTER the new frames are written to buffers
1018   // to prevent race condition where CLEAR arrives before new frame is ready
1019   uint64_t previous_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_previous_active_video_count);
1020   if (sources_with_video != (int)previous_count) {
1021     // Use compare-and-swap to ensure only ONE thread detects the change
1022     if (atomic_cas_u64(&g_previous_active_video_count, &previous_count, (uint64_t)sources_with_video)) {
1023       log_dev_every(
1024           LOG_RATE_DEFAULT,
1025           "Grid layout changing: %d -> %d active video sources - caller will broadcast clear AFTER buffering frame",
1026           previous_count, sources_with_video);
1027       if (out_grid_changed) {
1028         *out_grid_changed = true; // Signal to caller
1029       }
1030     }
1031   }
1032 
1033   // No active video sources - don't generate placeholder frames
1034   image_t *composite = NULL;
1035 
1036   if (sources_with_video == 0) {
1037     *out_size = 0;
1038     // No active video sources for client - this isn't an error.
1039     // Return NULL to indicate no frame should be sent
1040     return NULL;
1041   }
1042 
1043   START_TIMER("composite_create");
1044   if (sources_with_video == 1) {
1045     // Single source handling - create composite and convert to ASCII
1046     // Note: create_single_source_composite returns a reference to sources[i].image
1047     // which could be modified by other threads. Make a copy to prevent concurrent
1048     // modification during ascii_convert_with_capabilities.
1049     image_t *single_source = create_single_source_composite(sources, source_count, target_client_id, width, height);
1050     if (single_source) {
1051       composite = image_new_copy(single_source);
1052       if (!composite) {
1053         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to copy single source composite");
1054         *out_size = 0;
1055         return NULL;
1056       }
1057     }
1058   } else {
1059     // Multiple sources - create grid layout
1060     composite =
1061         create_multi_source_composite(sources, source_count, sources_with_video, target_client_id, width, height);
1062   }
1063   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 500000, "composite_create", "Composite creation (single/multi)");
1064 
1065   char *out = NULL;
1066 
1067   if (!composite) {
1068     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Per-client %s: Failed to create composite image", target_client_id);
1069     *out_size = 0;
1070     out = NULL;
1071   }
1072 
1073   // Convert composite to ASCII using client capabilities
1074   // Pass terminal dimensions so the frame can be padded to full width
1075   START_TIMER("ascii_convert");
1076   char *ascii_frame = convert_composite_to_ascii(composite, target_client_id, width, height);
1077   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 500000, "ascii_convert", "ASCII conversion (composite to ASCII)");
1078 
1079   if (ascii_frame) {
1080     // The frame should have been null-terminated by the padding functions.
1081     // Use strlen() which is optimized and reliable
1082     size_t ascii_len = strlen(ascii_frame);
1083 
1084     // Safety check: don't accept unreasonably large frames (10MB limit)
1085     if (ascii_len > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
1086       log_error("Frame size exceeds 10MB safety limit (possible buffer overflow)");
1087       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Frame size exceeds 10MB");
1088       return NULL;
1089     }
1090 
1091     // Ensure frame ends with a reset sequence to avoid garbage at terminal
1092     // This prevents color codes from leaking into uninitialized memory
1093     const char reset_seq[] = "\033[0m";
1094     const size_t reset_len = 4;
1095 
1096     if (ascii_len >= reset_len) {
1097       // Check if frame already ends with reset
1098       const char *frame_end = ascii_frame + ascii_len - reset_len;
1099       if (strncmp(frame_end, reset_seq, reset_len) == 0) {
1100         // Frame properly ends with reset, use full length
1101         *out_size = ascii_len;
1102       } else {
1103         // Frame doesn't end with reset - this is the REAL bug!
1104         // Truncate to the last occurrence of reset sequence
1105         const char *last_reset = NULL;
1106         for (const char *p = ascii_frame + ascii_len - reset_len; p >= ascii_frame; p--) {
1107           if (strncmp(p, reset_seq, reset_len) == 0) {
1108             last_reset = p;
1109             break;
1110           }
1111         }
1112 
1113         if (last_reset) {
1114           // Include the reset sequence and truncate after it
1115           *out_size = (size_t)(last_reset - ascii_frame) + reset_len;
1116           ascii_frame[*out_size] = '\0'; // Ensure null termination
1117           log_warn("Frame was missing reset at end (had garbage), truncated from %zu to %zu bytes", ascii_len,
1118                    *out_size);
1119         } else {
1120           // No reset found, use full length as fallback
1121           *out_size = ascii_len;
1122           log_warn("Frame has no reset sequences, sending full %zu bytes", ascii_len);
1123         }
1124       }
1125     } else {
1126       // Frame too short to have a reset, use as-is
1127       *out_size = ascii_len;
1128     }
1129 
1130     log_dev_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "create_mixed_ascii_frame_for_client: Final frame size=%zu bytes for client %u",
1131                   *out_size, target_client_id);
1132 
1133     // Debug: Log the last 50 bytes of the frame to see what's really there
1134     if (*out_size >= 50) {
1135       char hex_buf[300] = {0};
1136       size_t hex_len = 0;
1137       const uint8_t *last_bytes = (const uint8_t *)ascii_frame + (*out_size - 50);
1138       for (int i = 0; i < 50 && hex_len < sizeof(hex_buf) - 5; i++) {
1139         hex_len += snprintf(hex_buf + hex_len, sizeof(hex_buf) - hex_len, "%02X ", last_bytes[i]);
1140       }
1141       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "FRAME_LAST_50_BYTES (hex): %s", hex_buf);
1142 
1143       // Also log as ASCII for readability
1144       char ascii_buf[100] = {0};
1145       for (int i = 0; i < 50 && i < (int)sizeof(ascii_buf) - 1; i++) {
1146         if (last_bytes[i] >= 32 && last_bytes[i] < 127) {
1147           ascii_buf[i] = (char)last_bytes[i];
1148         } else if (last_bytes[i] == '\n') {
1149           ascii_buf[i] = 'N';
1150         } else if (last_bytes[i] == '\0') {
1151           ascii_buf[i] = '0';
1152         } else {
1153           ascii_buf[i] = '.';
1154         }
1155       }
1156       log_dev_every(4500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "FRAME_LAST_50_ASCII: %s", ascii_buf);
1157     }
1158 
1159     out = ascii_frame;
1160   } else {
1161     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_TERMINAL, "Per-client %u: Failed to convert image to ASCII", target_client_id);
1162     *out_size = 0;
1163   }
1164 
1165   START_TIMER("image_cleanup");
1166   if (composite) {
1167     // For single source, composite is a malloc-allocated copy, not from pool
1168     // Check alloc method to determine correct destroy function
1169     if (composite->alloc_method == IMAGE_ALLOC_POOL) {
1170       image_destroy_to_pool(composite);
1171     } else {
1172       image_destroy(composite);
1173     }
1174   }
1175   for (int i = 0; i < source_count; i++) {
1176     if (sources[i].image) {
1177       image_destroy_to_pool(sources[i].image);
1178     }
1179   }
1180   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(dev, 500000, "image_cleanup", "Image cleanup (composite + sources)");
1181 
1182   uint64_t frame_gen_end_ns = time_get_ns();
1183   uint64_t frame_gen_duration_ns = frame_gen_end_ns - frame_gen_start_ns;
1184   if (frame_gen_duration_ns > 10 * NS_PER_MS_INT) { // Log if > 10ms
1185     char duration_str[32];
1186     time_pretty((uint64_t)((double)frame_gen_duration_ns), -1, duration_str, sizeof(duration_str));
1187     log_warn("SLOW_FRAME_GENERATION: Client %u full frame gen took %s", target_client_id, duration_str);
1188   }
1189 
1190   return out;
1191 }






References image_t::alloc_method, atomic_cas_u64(),
    atomic_load_u64(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_TERMINAL,
    IMAGE_ALLOC_POOL, image_destroy(),
    image_destroy_to_pool(), image_new_copy(),
    log_dev_every, log_error, log_info,
    LOG_RATE_DEFAULT, LOG_RATE_SLOW, log_warn,
    MAX_CLIENTS, NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NS_PER_MS,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, SET_ERRNO, START_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG, time_get_ns(), time_pretty(), and
    US_PER_MS_INT.


Referenced by client_video_render_thread().




int
  queue_audio_for_client (client_info_t * client, const void *
  audio_data, size_t data_size)


Queue ASCII frame for delivery to specific client. Packages a
    generated ASCII frame into a protocol packet and queues it for asynchronous
    delivery to the target client. This function handles all protocol details
    including headers, checksums, and metadata.










PACKET
  STRUCTURE CREATED: 



  
•

  
ascii_frame_packet_t header containing:





  
 •

  
width, height: Client terminal dimensions

  
 •

  
original_size: Uncompressed frame size

  
 •

  
compressed_size: Compressed size (currently 0)

  
 •

  
checksum: CRC32 of frame data for integrity

  
 •

  
flags: Capability flags (color support, etc.)





  
•

  
Frame data: Complete ASCII art string with ANSI codes








PROTOCOL
  INTEGRATION: 


    
  
  • Converts dimensions to network byte order
    • 
  
  • Generates CRC32 checksum for error detection
    • 
  
  • Sets appropriate capability flags based on client
    • 
  
  • Uses PACKET_TYPE_ASCII_FRAME packet type
    • 







DELIVERY
  MECHANISM: 


    
  
  • Queues packet in client's video packet queue
    • 
  
  • Send thread delivers packet asynchronously
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow handling prevents memory exhaustion
    • 
  
  • Error conditions are logged but don't affect other clients
    • 







MEMORY MANAGEMENT: 


    
  
  • Creates temporary packet buffer for header+data combination
    • 
  
  • packet_queue_enqueue() copies data (safe to free temp buffer)
    • 
  
  • No memory leaks on success or failure paths
    • 
  
  • Buffer pool allocation not used here (temporary buffer)
    • 







ERROR HANDLING: 


    
  
  • Invalid parameters: Return -1, no side effects
    • 
  
  • Memory allocation failure: Logged, return -1
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow: Logged as debug (expected under load)
    • 
  
  • Client shutdown: Graceful failure without error spam
    • 



Parameters


client Target client for frame delivery


ascii_frame Generated ASCII art string (null-terminated)


frame_size Size of ASCII frame in bytes


Returns


0 on successful queuing, -1 on error


Note


Frame delivery happens asynchronously via send thread



Queue overflow drops frames to maintain real-time performance




Checksums enable client-side integrity verification




See also


packet_queue_enqueue() For underlying queue
  implementation



asciichat_crc32() For checksum generation




Queue audio data for delivery to specific client




Queues mixed audio data for delivery to a specific client. This is
    a simple wrapper around the packet queue system for audio delivery.








AUDIO
  PIPELINE INTEGRATION: 


    
  
  • Called by audio mixing threads after combining multiple client
    streams
    • 
  
  • Audio data is already in final format (float samples, mixed)
    • 
  
  • No additional processing required at this stage
    • 







PACKET
  DETAILS: 


    
  
  • Uses PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_BATCH packet type
    • 
  
  • Audio data is raw float samples bundled together
    • 
  
  • Batch format reduces packet overhead ~32x
    • 
  
  • Sample rate and format determined by audio system
    • 







DELIVERY
  CHARACTERISTICS: 


    
  
  • Higher priority than video packets (lower latency)
    • 
  
  • Uses client's audio packet queue
    • 
  
  • Send thread prioritizes audio over video
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow drops oldest audio to maintain real-time
    • 







ERROR HANDLING: 


    
  
  • Invalid parameters: Return -1 immediately
    • 
  
  • Queue overflow: Handled by packet queue (drops old data)
    • 
  
  • Client shutdown: Graceful failure
    • 



Parameters


client Target client for audio delivery


audio_data Mixed audio samples (float format)


data_size Size of audio data in bytes


Returns


0 on successful queuing, -1 on error


Note


Audio has priority over video in send thread



Real-time audio requires queue overflow handling




See also


packet_queue_enqueue() For underlying queue
  implementation



mixer.c For Audio mixing implementation




Definition at line 1301 of file stream.c.


1301                                                                                             {
1302   if (!client || !client->audio_queue || !audio_data || data_size == 0) {
1303     return -1;
1304   }
1305 
1306   return packet_queue_enqueue(client->audio_queue, PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_BATCH, audio_data, data_size, 0, true);
1307 }






References client_info::audio_queue,
    packet_queue_enqueue(), and PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_BATCH.






Variable
  Documentation




atomic_t g_should_exit [extern]


Global application exit flag (shared across all modes). Global
    shutdown flag referenced by lib/logging.c and lib/debug/lock.c In production
    builds, this is defined in main.c and set during shutdown. For tests, we
    stub it out as false (tests don't perform shutdown).


Definition at line 34 of file globals.c.


34 {0};






Referenced by ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO(),
    client_audio_render_thread(), client_dispatch_thread(),
    client_receive_thread(), client_send_thread_func(),
    client_video_render_thread(),
    handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), handle_image_frame_packet(),
    server_main(), should_exit(), signal_exit(),
    stats_logger_thread(), and stop_client_render_threads().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.11.2
    ascii-chat
